---
title: "Automated Tests Guide (v0.0.3)"
---

<!--
DOC STRUCTURE RULES
- Exactly one H1 (#) heading at the top
- All other headings must be ## or deeper
- Do NOT hard-code section numbers
-->

**Quick reference — run tests and confirm they pass**

- **Run tests (watch mode):** `npm run test`
- **Run tests once (CI / pre-commit):** `npm run test:run`
- **All pass:** Exit code is 0 and terminal shows no failed tests. Use `npm run test:run` before committing or pushing.

**Stack:** Vitest. Unit tests live next to source in `src/**/*.test.ts` (and `*.test.tsx` if added). See [TECH-STACK-0.0.3-v001.md](TECH-STACK-0.0.3-v001.md) for project stack.

---

# Automated Tests Guide — yrS3 v0.0.3 {#automated-tests-guide}

This guide describes the automated test setup for regression prevention: how tests are organized, what to test first, how to keep tests robust (especially when using AI coding agents), and how to run them. It is written for both human developers and LLM coding tools.

**Related:** [DEVELOPER-GUIDE-0.0.1-v001.qmd](DEVELOPER-GUIDE-0.0.1-v001.qmd), [TECH-STACK-0.0.3-v001.md](TECH-STACK-0.0.3-v001.md).

---

## Overview {#sec-overview}

- **Goal:** Prevent regression bugs via automated tests. Tests are the primary guardrail when refactoring or when AI agents modify code.
- **Runner:** Vitest (unit and API-level tests). Optional later: Playwright for E2E.
- **Layout:** Colocated unit tests in `src/` (e.g. `src/lib/questions/version.test.ts`). API route tests use mocks for auth and Supabase.
- **Priority order:** (1) Pure lib logic (version, validate, parse, evaluators), (2) API routes with mocks, (3) optional E2E for critical flows.

---

## How to run tests {#sec-run}

| Action | Command |
|--------|--------|
| Run tests in watch mode | `npm run test` |
| Run once (e.g. CI, pre-commit) | `npm run test:run` |
| Confirm all pass | Run `npm run test:run`; exit code 0 and no failures in output |

No environment variables are required for unit tests. API route tests mock Supabase and auth.

---

## Test scope and priorities {#sec-scope}

### Phase 1: Unit tests (pure logic)

High-value, no-mock modules under `src/lib/`:

- **`src/lib/questions/version.ts`** — `parseVersion`, `isValidVersion`, `compareVersion`, `formatVersion`, `bumpVersion`. Cover valid/invalid strings, comparison order, each bump type.
- **`src/lib/questions/validate.ts`** — `validateMetaYaml`, `validateOptionsYaml`, type-specific validators. Use inline fixture objects.
- **`src/lib/questions/parse.ts`** — `parseQuestion`, prompt selection. Use in-memory `QuestionFile[]` arrays.
- **`src/lib/evaluators/short-answer.ts`** and **`multiple-choice.ts`** — `evaluateShortAnswer`, `evaluateMultipleChoice`. Cover expected/answers, normalization, correct vs wrong.
- **`src/app/components/tag-filter-bar-load.ts`** — `fetchAvailableTags`. Tag filter bar loads from GET /api/tags (taxonomy) and falls back to GET /api/questions/tags when the taxonomy is unavailable. Tests in `tag-filter-bar-load.test.ts` mock `fetch` and cover: taxonomy array of `{ path }`, flat string array, non-OK response fallback, non-JSON body fallback, non-array JSON fallback, and failure cases. See [SPEC-0.0.8](SPEC-0.0.8-HIERARCHICAL-TAGS-AND-CANONICAL-FACTS.qmd) UI section.

### Phase 2: API route tests

- Call route handlers with constructed `NextRequest` and params. Mock `requireUser` and Supabase (e.g. `vi.mock("@/lib/auth")`, `vi.mock("@/lib/supabase/server")`).
- Assert: invalid input → 400 and stable error shape; unauthenticated → 401 (or your standard); valid input → 200/201 and minimal response fields.
- Example routes: `POST /api/questions/[id]/versions`, `GET /api/questions/my`, question-set start/submit.

### Phase 3: CI and optional E2E

- CI: Run `npm run test:run` and `npm run lint` on every change; block merge on failure.
- E2E (later): Playwright for one or two critical user flows if needed.

---

## Keeping tests robust (anti-fragility) {#sec-robust}

These rules reduce brittleness and keep the suite useful when refactoring or when AI agents edit code.

### Test behavior, not implementation

- Assert on **inputs and outputs** (return values, thrown errors, HTTP status and body). Avoid asserting on internal function call order, exact log messages, or low-level DB query shapes.
- Prefer **coarse assertions**: check the few fields that matter (e.g. `status`, `error`, `data.id`) instead of snapshotting entire responses that change often.

### Minimize mocking

- Mock only at **boundaries**: auth and Supabase client. Avoid mocking internal modules or implementation details; that couples tests to refactors.
- Unit-test **pure functions** in `src/lib/` without mocks first; add API tests that mock only the external services.

### Stable, deterministic tests

- No real network, no real time, no unseeded randomness. If time is needed, freeze it in tests.
- One failing test should **point to one cause**: prefer small, single-purpose tests with clear names over long scenario tests that fail in obscure ways.

### Fixtures and snapshots

- If using fixture files (e.g. YAML), keep them **small and purpose-built** (one fixture per rule). Avoid large “kitchen sink” fixtures.
- Use **snapshots sparingly**; prefer explicit assertions on a few key fields. If you use snapshots, avoid updating them without reviewing the diff.

### Agent-friendly rules (for AI-assisted changes)

- **Definition of done:** Any change that touches behaviour must update or add tests; all tests must pass; no deleting or weakening tests without explicit request.
- **No silent weakening:** Do not remove tests, loosen assertions, or add broad `try/catch` that hides failures. If a test is wrong, fix or replace it explicitly.
- **Refactor rule:** When refactoring internals, most tests should stay unchanged. If many tests need changes, the tests may be tied to implementation rather than behaviour—prefer fixing tests to assert behaviour.
- **Invariants as tests:** Encode critical invariants (e.g. version sort order, validation error shape, prompt selection) as tests so they act as guardrails against accidental breakage.

---

## Configuration and layout {#sec-config}

- **Config file:** `vitest.config.mts` at repo root (ESM) so Vitest uses Vite’s ESM API and avoids the CJS deprecation warning. Scripts pass `--config vitest.config.mts`. Include `src/**/*.test.ts` and `src/**/*.test.tsx`; preserve path alias `@/*` → `./src/*`; exclude `node_modules` and `.next`.
- **Placement:** Colocated `*.test.ts` next to the module under test (e.g. `version.test.ts` beside `version.ts`).
- **Scripts:** `package.json` should define `"test": "vitest"` and `"test:run": "vitest run"`.

---

## Doc version {#sec-doc-version}

- **Doc:** AUTOMATED-TESTS-GUIDE-0.0.3-v001 (test strategy and robustness rules for release 0.0.3).
